sub_tasks.requirements: Method and code fidelity to the paper

train.py implements the sigmoid gate g_t = σ(β · Δ_t) with β=10 and applies a stop-gradient (detach) on the gate so gradients flow only through the student log-probability, as described in Section 2.3 and Appendix A Proposition 5.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The OPSD auxiliary loss is added on top of the unmodified GRPO policy loss as L = L_GRPO + λ · L_OPSD with λ=0.1 (the distillation weight identified as optimal in Section 3.3 / Figure 8 / Table 3), and the GRPO advantage computation is left untouched (Section 2.3).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The token-level Teacher-Student log-probability gap is computed as Δ_t = log π_teacher(y_t|·) − log π_student(y_t|·) using a single-sample estimate on the student-sampled token (not full-vocabulary), matching the reverse-KL-aligned definition in Section 2.2.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The code implements all three gating strategies from Section 2.3 — Entropy gating g = σ(β·H_t), Gap gating g = σ(β·Δ_t), and Soft-OR gating g = 1 − (1−σ(β·H_t))(1−σ(β·Δ_t)) — and defaults to Gap gating per Section 3.3 Figure 6.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The five baselines — GRPO, OPSD, Skill-SD, GRPO+OPSD, and RLSD — are re-implemented per Appendix Algorithms 2–6 with the same Qwen backbone as SDAR, and Skill-GRPO / Skill-Prompt use keyword-matching (KM) skill retrieval as described in Section 3.1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The four skill retrieval strategies from Section 2.1 are implemented: UCB Retrieval using the UCB criterion in Eq. 1, Keyword Matching against predefined category labels, Full Retrieval, and Random Retrieval, against the SkillBank from SkillRL (Section 3).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Data and preprocessing fidelity

ALFWorld training uses the data split from GiGPO (Feng et al. 2025) with the 6 task categories (Pick, Look, Clean, Heat, Cool, Pick2) over the 3,827 task instances of Shridhar et al. 2020, as specified in Section 3.

Search-QA training data are drawn from NQ + HotpotQA (in-domain), with TriviaQA, PopQA, 2Wiki, MuSiQue, and Bamboogle held out as out-of-domain evaluation, using E5 (Wang et al. 2022) as the retriever per Search-R1 setup (Section 3).
WebShop training uses 1000 selected tasks and evaluates on the 128 fixed validation tasks aligned with Feng et al. 2025 (Section 3).

The prompt templates for ALFWorld, Search-QA, and WebShop (Appendix Figures 15–17) populate {skill_context} with the retrieved skill at training time and leave it empty at inference time (Appendix C).
Experiment execution and reproducibility
Training is executed for 150 steps on the Qwen2.5-Instruct and Qwen3-Instruct backbones (specifically Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, and Qwen3-1.7B-Instruct) as stated in Section 3.

ALFWorld and WebShop runs use batch size 16 tasks × 8 rollouts per prompt; Search-QA uses batch size 128 tasks; maximum prompt length is 2048 for ALFWorld and 4096 for Search-QA and WebShop (Section 3).

GRPO hyperparameters from Appendix Table 3 are set in code: group size G, PPO clip ratio ε, KL penalty coefficient α_KL toward the reference policy, with learning rate η matching Table 3 values (Section 3 / Appendix Table 3).
Hardware configuration matches Section 3: training executed on 8× H800 GPUs (or an equivalent multi-GPU setup the artifact documents as a substitution) for the Qwen2.5/Qwen3 runs.

An end-to-end runnable script is provided that reproduces SDAR training on at least one (model, environment) pair from Table 1 and emits checkpoints + metrics logs, with seeds fixed for reproducibility.
Evaluation protocol and metric correctness

Training diagnostics tracked match Section 3.2 / Appendix Figures 10–14: gate activation ratio (fraction of tokens where g_t > 0.5), mean gate value, OPSD loss, mean Teacher-Student gap Δ_t, and reward curve.

The Skill-GRPO baseline is evaluated both with skills at test time (Skill-GRPO*) and without skills, while SDAR is evaluated without external skills at inference, matching the protocol in Section 3.1.

Robustness evaluation reports SDAR results across all four retrieval tiers (UCB, KM, Full, Random) on ALFWorld and WebShop (Score/Acc) and compares against the GRPO baseline (w/o OPSD), as in Table 2.
Result match versus the paper's reported targets
SDAR improves over GRPO on Qwen2.5-3B by approximately +9.4% on ALFWorld (84.4 vs. 75.0), +7.0% on Search-QA, and +4.7% on WebShop-Acc, as reported in the abstract and Section 3.1 / Table 1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On Qwen3-1.7B, SDAR reaches approximately 53.9% on ALFWorld, strictly outperforming GRPO (46.1), Skill-GRPO (21.1), and RLSD (42.2), reproducing the gap reported in Section 3.1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduction confirms the failure modes reported in Section 3.1: standalone OPSD collapses to near-zero on Search-QA, and naive GRPO+OPSD on Qwen3-1.7B degrades to ≈32.0 vs. GRPO's 46.1 on ALFWorld.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation reproduces the β sweep result that β=10 is optimal in Section 3.3 / Figure 7, with β=0 (no gate) and β→large (hard binarization) both underperforming β=10 on Qwen2.5-3B ALFWorld.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation reproduces the λ sweep finding from Section 3.3 / Figure 8 that λ=0.1 is optimal, with λ=1.0 causing severe performance decline and λ=0.01 providing insufficient distillation signal on Qwen2.5-3B.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Artifact completeness and provenance

The repository at https://github.com/ZJU-REAL/SDAR (cited in the abstract) is present or mirrored, including the SDAR algorithm implementation (Appendix Algorithm 1) and the five baseline algorithms (Algorithms 2–6).
A configuration file or table documents the per-method hyperparameters listed in Appendix Table 3 (η, G, ε, λ=0.1, β=10, α_KL, SRS=KM) for SDAR and each baseline.
Final metrics JSON or table reports per-model (Qwen2.5-3B, Qwen2.5-7B, Qwen3-1.7B) × per-environment (ALFWorld, Search-QA, WebShop) results in the same layout as Table 1.
Training diagnostic plots equivalent to Appendix Figures 10–14 (gate active ratio, gate mean, OPSD loss, Teacher-Student gap, reward curve) are saved as artifacts for at least one full training run.
